name: apple-reminders-blade-mcp
title: Apple Reminders
description: Apple Reminders operations — lists, reminders, search, due dates, create, complete, update, delete
tagline: Native Apple Reminders access on macOS
author: groupthink-dev
notes: macOS only. Requires Reminders permission (auto-prompted on first AppleScript access).

tools.name: reminders_info
tools.description: System info: macOS version, Reminders access status, list count, write gate state.

tools.name: reminders_lists
tools.description: List all reminder lists with id, name, reminder count.

tools.name: reminders_items
tools.description: Get reminders with server-side list_name + completed filters + limit pushed into the AppleScript whose-clause.

tools.name: reminders_item
tools.description: Get a single reminder by reminder_id or by name (optionally scoped to a list).

tools.name: reminders_search
tools.description: Search reminder titles across all lists with limit. Match is AppleScript-side; full corpus enumerated then filtered in-script.

tools.name: reminders_due
tools.description: Reminders due within N days. AppleScript enumerates all lists; due-date comparison pushed into the script.

tools.name: reminders_stats
tools.description: Aggregate statistics: total, completed, incomplete, overdue, per-list breakdown.

tools.name: reminders_create
tools.description: Create a new reminder. Write — gated by REMINDERS_WRITE_ENABLED + confirm.

tools.name: reminders_complete
tools.description: Mark a reminder completed/uncompleted. Write — gated by REMINDERS_WRITE_ENABLED + confirm.

tools.name: reminders_update
tools.description: Update reminder properties (title, due_date, priority, notes, flagged). Write — gated by REMINDERS_WRITE_ENABLED + confirm.

tools.name: reminders_delete
tools.description: Delete a reminder. Destructive — gated by REMINDERS_WRITE_ENABLED + confirm; cannot be undone.

setup.blurb: Read and manage Apple Reminders — lists, due dates, search, create, complete, and update reminders on macOS.
setup.help_text: Grant Reminders access when macOS prompts, or enable it in System Settings > Privacy & Security > Reminders for your terminal emulator.

fields.label: Enable Write Operations
fields.help: Set to 'true' to enable creating, completing, updating, and deleting reminders. All writes require additional confirmation.

prerequisites: Reminders permission for terminal (auto-prompted on first use)

highlights.text: Lists — browse all reminder lists with item counts
Search — find reminders by keyword across all lists
Due dates — view overdue and upcoming reminders
Create — add reminders with due dates, priorities, notes
Stats — summary counts across lists and completion rates
Write-gated — create, complete, update, delete require opt-in

readme:
### What it does

Apple Reminders gives your AI agent native access to the Reminders app on macOS. Browse lists, search for reminders, check due dates, and manage tasks — all through the system Reminders database via AppleScript.

Your agent can see what's overdue, find reminders by keyword, get list-level stats, and create or complete reminders without switching to the app.

### macOS integration

This plugin talks directly to Apple Reminders through AppleScript — no API keys, no cloud setup. Just grant Reminders access when macOS prompts (System Settings > Privacy & Security > Reminders). Changes sync automatically via iCloud to all your Apple devices.

### Safety model

Read operations work immediately — lists, search, due dates, stats, individual reminder details.

Write operations (create, complete, update, delete) require `REMINDERS_WRITE_ENABLED=true` **and** `confirm: true` on each call. Double-gated by design, so your agent can browse freely but must be explicit about changes.

### Tool coverage

| Category | Tools | What you can do |
|----------|-------|-----------------|
| Read | 6 | Lists, items, search, due dates, stats, detail |
| Write | 4 | Create, complete, update, delete (all gated) |

scenarios.label: Triage overdue reminders
scenarios.body: Fetch all overdue reminders across lists, review each item's priority and notes, then complete finished items or update due dates for those that need rescheduling.

scenarios.label: Capture tasks from a meeting
scenarios.body: Create reminders from a list of action items with appropriate due dates and priorities, assigning each to the correct reminder list.

scenarios.label: Weekly reminder review
scenarios.body: Get stats across all lists, identify lists with the most incomplete items, then search for reminders due this week to plan your priorities.
